findings.message: New Dashboard view ships a fetch to /internal/summary with no RUM/error instrumentation reachable from it. The promise chain (.then(r => r.json()).then(setData)) has no error handling: a failed request, non-2xx response, or JSON parse failure leaves `data` null forever with no user-visible error state and no explicit RUM error signal — the failure of this user-facing code path is effectively unobserved. The global unhandledrejection handler only emits a generic, undifferentiated error count that cannot identify this view or fetch.
findings.suggestion: Add a .catch that records an explicit RUM signal for this fetch (e.g. push a kind:"error" event distinguishing the summary fetch, or route through the existing instrumented fetch wrapper) and surface a user-visible error/loading-failed state, so a broken /internal/summary load is observable rather than silently blank.
